real gateway :8907: GET /login renders the styled form; wrong password Ôćĺ NO set-cookie; correct Ôćĺ 302 + signed cookie

env -u SPEXCODE_PASSWORD (first run false-red: shell INHERITED the deploy password ÔÇö precedence worked as spec'd): startup prints the loud OPEN warning, / and /api/board both 200 uncookied, no login gate
re-measured at HEAD (:8907): /api/board 246778BÔćĺ71172B gzip (28.8%); SSE stream 0 content-encoding headers; rename event 0.21s through the gateway

Gate boundary itself correct (401/302/login/cookie/WS-gate all as expected) BUT the child backend binds *:PORT ÔÇö reachable from the LAN with no password, bypassing the gateway entirely. Spec says supervisor+child stay on 127.0.0.1.

Post-fix (child serve() now binds 127.0.0.1): full boundary as specced ÔÇö 401/302/login-error/httpOnly+Secure per-port cookie/200s with cookie/forged 401/WS destroyed without cookie + 101 with; only the public port is non-loopback, child refused from LAN.

board 434563Ôćĺ126270 (29%), dist JS 898183Ôćĺ262749 (29%), both Content-Encoding: gzip only when accepted; SSE stream carries NO content-encoding and the triggered board-changed arrived ~5.4s after touch = debounce + a 2.4s board rebuild on this loaded box (build-time over budget, not a zlib stall).

Loud OPEN warning at startup + gateway logs 'OPEN (no password)'; unauth GET / Ôćĺ 200 dashboard (not 302), /api/board Ôćĺ 200 JSON, login layer absent (/login falls through to the dashboard, no card, no cookie check). Run with SPEXCODE_PASSWORD unset ÔÇö an inherited env password correctly gates (env is a designed source).
